pi_items.source: Calculations via Skyfield using the JPL DE421 planetary ephemeris precisely yield the specified 02:30 and 03:30 ingress/egress times.

privileged_info_summary: The task instructions and target celestial event are grounded in reality. The lunar occultation of Mars occurred on December 8, 2022. Using the JPL DE421 ephemeris with the coordinates for Griffith Observatory (34.118 N, 118.300 W, altitude 346m), calculations confirm the apparent separation between the Moon and Mars centers reaches approximately 0.25 degrees (the Moon's angular radius) at 02:30 UTC (ingress) and again at 03:30 UTC (egress). The export script strictly verifies the observation plan keywords, expected output paths, new screenshot counts, and Stellarium configuration flags (atmosphere off, landscape off, equatorial grid on).
